reason: Both answers correctly land on very worried and cover the same core prescriptions: telemetry detection, remote config for fast patching, fallback selectors, canary monitoring, checking the official Data API, diversifying acquisition, and capturing users into a direct account or email relationship. Both correctly flag the Chrome Web Store remote-code policy constraint. Answer A adds more concrete engineering specifics for resilient selectors (ARIA roles, stable custom element tags, MutationObservers for SPA navigation) plus a concrete breakage-frequency estimate. Answer B adds a distinct risk category, Chrome Web Store policy action or deliberate anti-scraping countermeasures, framing DOM breakage as survivable versus delisting as not, sharpening the diversification rationale. Neither contains a misleading error, and the unique contributions roughly offset, making this a genuine tie.
